seed_problem: remove at most k links from a themed grid network to maximize the s-t shortest-path (delay) while keeping s and t connected
mutation: output-constrained interdiction: keep the max-shortest-path goal but require the residual network stay s-t connected and cap the number of severed links at k (budget-constrained most-vital-edges, NP-hard)
title: Interstellar Relay Sabotage: Maximum Signal Delay Under a Cut Budget
strategies: do-nothing baseline: sever no links (residual delay == original, calibration point)
cheapest-on-path greedy: iteratively cut the lowest-latency link on the current shortest route while preserving connectivity
steepest-ascent interdiction: each round cut the path link whose removal maximizes the resulting shortest-path delay
bottleneck/cut-oriented search: target links shared by many short routes or forming a narrow crossing to force expensive detours
